summary: Matched a large set of ftKirby Game & Watch neutral-special callback and transition routines, replacing many placeholder asm markers in ftKb_Init.c with C implementations. The PR also fixed/filled related callback wiring for Kirby's copied Peach and Game & Watch specials, added item headers for Game & Watch chef/pan interactions, and refined ftKirby fighter var field types for the copied Peach toad and Game & Watch pan item handles.
what_changed: Added includes for itgamewatchchef.h and itkirbygamewatchchefpan.h so ftKirby Game & Watch copied-special code can call the relevant item routines directly.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C3F4, which clears Kirby Peach-special item state by calling it_802BDF40 on fp->fv.kb.xD0 and then ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C3C0.
Changed ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C47C to call it_802BDFC0 instead of it_802BDFA0 when fp->fv.kb.xD0 is present.
Implemented multiple Game & Watch copied-special item callbacks around fp->fv.kb.xDC, including pan spawn/setup, cleanup, hitlag enter/exit item forwarding, and action-state validity checks.
Implemented grounded and aerial Game & Watch copied neutral-special animation callbacks, IASA callbacks, collision callbacks, and ground/air transition helpers.
Implemented repeat-loop helpers ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D660 and ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D6D0 with an f32 frame argument used to restart the motion at specialn_gw_frame_on_repeat - 1.0F.
Added a shared inline helper that resets cmd_vars[1], cmd_vars[2], isChefLoopDisable, and accessory4_cb when repeating the Game & Watch chef loop.
Updated ftKb_Init.h declarations by adding fn_8010D100 and changing ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D660/ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D6D0 prototypes to accept a float argument.
Updated ftKirby/types.h field knowledge: xD0 is documented as a Peach toad Item_GObj pointer, xDC as a Game & Watch pan Item_GObj pointer, and xD4/xD8 as int rather than UNK_T.
smart_moves: Reused existing Game & Watch copied-special state variables and callbacks rather than introducing new state, preserving the original callback-driven item lifecycle pattern.
Modeled the Game & Watch pan as an Item_GObj pointer in ftKb_FighterVars, which made null checks and item callback forwarding explicit and type-safe.

Kept ground and air versions parallel: animation callbacks differ only in repeat target and ending action, IASA callbacks share the same B-button loop-disable logic, and repeat helpers share an inline reset routine.
Used the fighter's left thumb bone via ftParts_GetBoneIndex(fp, FtPart_LThumbNb) and lb_8000B1CC to spawn the Kirby Game & Watch pan item at the correct bone-derived position.
Preserved callback cleanup conventions: pan item callbacks are nulled/forwarded through death, damage, pre-hitlag, post-hitlag, and accessory callbacks depending on item lifetime.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in matched functions, indicating stack-shape adjustments were necessary for matching.
decomp_lessons: Copied-character special moves may need both fighter callback matching and item lifecycle matching; the item handle fields in fighter vars should be typed once their meaning is known.

When matching callback-heavy fighter code, identify the item pointer field first, then implement the clear, remove, pre-hitlag, post-hitlag, and accessory callback functions as a coherent lifecycle set.
For repeated animation loops, command variables can be the key control path: here cmd_vars[1] and cmd_vars[2] gate B-button repeat behavior and chef-loop continuation.
Ground/air transition helpers can preserve animation continuity by passing fp->cur_anim_frame into Fighter_ChangeMotionState and restoring accessory callbacks afterward.
Header prototypes must be updated when previously unknown functions are discovered to take additional frame/timing parameters.
Inline helpers can be useful for matching repeated small state-reset sequences across ground and air variants.
naming_conventions: Address-based ftKirby copied-special names were retained, e.g. ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D660 and ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D6D0.
Game & Watch copied-special functions use the ftKb_SpecialNGw and ftKb_GwSpecialN/GwSpecialAirN naming pattern.
Unidentified local callback functions still use fn_ address names such as fn_8010CE5C, fn_8010CFB0, and fn_8010D100.
Fighter var fields remain offset-style names, but comments document discovered item ownership: xD0 is Peach toad item and xDC is Game & Watch pan item.
The shared helper is named generically as inline1, signaling a matching-oriented inline rather than a semantically finalized API name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(0x20) in the Game & Watch animation callbacks and PAD_STACK(0x8) in IASA/repeat helpers to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Used a forward declaration for fn_8010CE5C before assigning it as accessory4_cb from fn_8010CFB0, avoiding declaration-order issues while preserving function order.
Repeated motion restart functions call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with arg1 - 1.0F and then ftAnim_8006EBA4, matching the frame-repeat behavior implied by specialn_gw_frame_on_repeat.
The ground/air repeat functions share a static inline helper to reproduce identical state-reset code in both call sites.

Condition ordering in ground and air animation callbacks is kept slightly different but equivalent around maxSausage and isChefLoopDisable, likely because source-level expression ordering affected matching.
Motion-state transition flags such as 0x0C4C508C were preserved as constants in the matched helpers.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR appears to have merged quickly with one issue comment recorded but no review-comment excerpt available, so reviewer rationale cannot be inferred from the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type ftKirby fv.kb.xD0/xDC fields.
Search for equivalent ftGameWatch_SpecialN implementations to compare callback structure and possible naming upgrades.
Look for PRs matching other Kirby copied-special item lifecycles using death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, pre_hitlag_cb, post_hitlag_cb, and accessory4_cb.
Check whether fn_8010CE5C, fn_8010CFB0, and fn_8010D100 receive semantic names in later cleanup PRs.
Search for use of PAD_STACK in ftKirby callbacks to identify other matching-sensitive copied-special routines.
